refnamediv.refpurpose: Interactively render a 3-D image volume using WebGL.

refsynopsisdiv.title: Syntax
refsynopsisdiv.synopsis: viewer = volshow(volume) viewer = volshow(dicomFilename) viewer = volshow(volume, options) viewer = volshow(volume, dicomInfo) viewer = volshow(volume, options, dicomInfo)

listitem.para: A non-empty scalar hypermatrix indexed as volume(row, column, slice).
Filename of an uncompressed grayscale DICOM image or multi-frame volume supported by dicomread.

Optional structure. Supported fields are RenderingStyle ("volume", "mip", "isosurface", "slices", or "2d"), Colormap ("gray", "bone", "hot", or "turbo"), Opacity, normalized Threshold, texture Quality from 32 through 256, intensity Window as [low high], VoxelSpacing as [row column slice], and Title.
Optional metadata structure returned by dicomread. Pixel spacing, slice spacing, intensity window, photometric interpretation, modality, and series description are used when available.
Structure containing the Scilab figure handle, source and texture dimensions, intensity range, voxel spacing, physical size, rendering style, and temporary runtime directory.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: volshow opens IPCV's interactive GPU volume renderer in a Scilab browser uicontrol. It uses WebGL 2 ray marching rather than a thresholded point cloud. The viewer supports front-to-back volume compositing, maximum-intensity projection, shaded isosurfaces, and three orthogonal slice planes, and a dedicated 2-D layer viewer.

Drag the volume to rotate it, use the mouse wheel to zoom, and double-click to reset the camera. Controls adjust the transfer threshold, opacity, colormap, sampling quality, slice positions, clipping extents, and a display-only Z scale. Actual spacing restores the physical DICOM aspect ratio. Equal voxels ignores voxel anisotropy for inspection without changing the volume or its metadata. In the 2-D viewer, choose an axial, coronal, or sagittal plane and use the layer slider or mouse wheel to move through the volume. Enter a Scilab variable name and select Export layer to place the current full-resolution layer, with its original datatype, in the Scilab workspace. The displayed frame can also be exported as PNG.

The 3-D processing panel applies IPCV operations to the current full-resolution volume and refreshes the GPU preview. It provides intensity adjustment, box, Gaussian and median filtering, gradient magnitude, normalized thresholding, regional maxima, erosion, dilation, opening, closing, cavity filling, small-component removal, surface-voxel extraction, and k-means segmentation. Undo exchanges the current and previous result, Original restores the input volume, and Export places the complete current volume in the Scilab workspace.

The native staging gateway normalizes the selected intensity window to 8-bit and proportionally downsamples volumes whose largest dimension exceeds Quality. Rendering therefore remains responsive without changing the original volume. Physical voxel spacing controls the displayed aspect ratio. DICOM in-plane distance comes from PixelSpacing; layer distance comes from SpacingBetweenSlices, falling back to SliceThickness. The viewer reports the selected source, true physical extent, and scaled display extent. A short partial stack can therefore appear physically thin even when spacing is correct. Image rows retain the IPCV convention that their origin is at the upper-left.

refsection.title: Examples

tr.td: Interactive WebGL volume rendering, scrollable 2-D layers, and Scilab workspace export introduced.
